quest.storage.controller.desc_1: The &bController&r acts as the battery for the refined storage system, and can be powered from any side. It will light up if powered correctly.
quest.storage.controller.desc_2: You can check how much power a certain part of a system uses by &6[Right-Clicking]&r the controller block.
quest.storage.controller.desc_3: &cNOTE:&r There can only be &cone&r controller per Refined Storage Network.
quest.storage.controller.desc_4: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.storage.controller.subtitle: Shiny...
quest.storage.controller.title: The Controller
quest.storage.cores.desc_1: &bConstruction&r and &cDestruction&r Cores are used in blocks that interact with the world (importers/exporters/grids).
quest.storage.cores.desc_2: You will need at least one of each if you're building the system from scratch, as they are used to make the Grid.
quest.storage.cores.title: Construction and Destruction Cores
quest.storage.craftinggrid.desc_1: The &bCrafting Grid&r allows access to a crafting table, as well as enabling the storing and retrieing of &eitems&r from the Refined Storage Network.
quest.storage.craftinggrid.desc_2: Place it next to the &bcontroller&r, or connected via cable.
quest.storage.craftinggrid.desc_3: &6[Right-Click]&r to open up the crafting menu.
quest.storage.craftinggrid.desc_4: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.storage.craftinggrid.subtitle: The power of creation in the palm of your hand.
quest.storage.craftinggrid.title: The Crafting Grid
quest.storage.discdrive.desc_1: The &bDisk Drive&r can fit up to &68&r storage disks, which determine how many items your storage system can hold.
quest.storage.discdrive.desc_2: Place it next to the &bcontroller&r, or connected via cable.
quest.storage.discdrive.desc_3: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.storage.discdrive.subtitle: You'd think we found something better than floppy disks...
quest.storage.discdrive.title: The Disk Drive
quest.storage.exporter.desc_1: &bExporters&r will automatically &esend items&r from the storage system into the inventory it is connected to.
quest.storage.exporter.desc_2: Unlike importers, exporters will not export items unless you tell it what to send by placing the desired item in its filter slot.
quest.storage.exporter.title: Exporting Items
quest.storage.fortuneupgrade.desc_1: Applies Fortune to the blocks that are destroyed by a &bDestructor&r.
quest.storage.fortuneupgrade.title: Fortune Upgrade
quest.storage.fortuneupgrade2.desc_1: Applies Fortune II to the blocks that are destroyed by a &bDestructor&r.
quest.storage.fortuneupgrade2.title: Fortune II Upgrade
quest.storage.fortuneupgrade3.desc_1: Applies Fortune III to the blocks that are destroyed by a &bDestructor&r.
quest.storage.fortuneupgrade3.title: Fortune III Upgrade
quest.storage.grid.desc_1: The &bGrid&r is required to craft the &bCrafting Grid&r. It allows for storing and retrieving &eitems&r from the network, but does &cnot&r include a crafting table.
quest.storage.grid.desc_2: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!§r
quest.storage.grid.subtitle: *tron music*
quest.storage.grid.title: The Grid
quest.storage.importer.desc_1: &bImporters&r will automatically &epull items&r from the inventory it is connected to into the storage system.
quest.storage.importer.desc_2: The items imported can be controlled by placing the desired items in its filter slot.
quest.storage.importer.title: Importing Items
quest.storage.patterngrid.desc_1: The &bPattern Grid&r allows players to store crafting recipies into &bPatterns&r, which can then be used in a &bCrafter&r.
quest.storage.patterngrid.desc_2: Players can chose to create &eExact&r or &eProcessing&r recipies.
quest.storage.patterngrid.desc_3: &eExact:&r Similar to vanilla crafting table recipes. You can specify a recipe to use from &bJEI&r by &6[Left-Clicking]&r on the item in JEI and clicking &eMove Items&r.
quest.storage.patterngrid.desc_4: &eProcessing:&r Works by making crafters send an input set of items and waiting for the desired output to be returned to the network.
quest.storage.patterngrid.desc_5: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.storage.patterngrid.subtitle: Automated Crafting, On Demand
quest.storage.patterngrid.title: The Pattern Grid
quest.storage.silicon.desc_1: Unlocking the more advanced features of Refined Storage requires a steady supply of &bProcessors&r.
quest.storage.silicon.desc_2: Making them by hand requires smelting &bSilicon&r using an &eArc Furnace&r, as well as a &ccomplex&r sequenced assembly chain.
quest.storage.silicon.desc_3: Due to this, it is recommended you focus on &elooting&r and &escrapping&r existing Refined Storage components using the &bScrapping Table§r.
quest.storage.silicon.subtitle: Now we're playing with technology.
quest.storage.silicon.title: Expanding your Network
quest.storage.storage_disks.desc_1: &bStorage Disks&r act as storage space for the Refined Storage Network, and can be placed in the &bDisk Drive&r.
quest.storage.storage_disks.desc_2: These disks are where your items are actually stored, so &cdon't lose them&r§r!
quest.storage.storage_disks.desc_3: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, it is far easier to loot it from &eT2 &7districts!
quest.storage.storage_disks.title: Storage Disks
quest.storage.storage_parts.desc_1: &bStorage Parts&r are used to craft Storage Disks, which determine the amount of items you can store in your storage system.
quest.storage.storage_parts.desc_2: They can be upgraded into higher tiers, which store more items: &e1k > 4k > 16k > 64k&r§r
quest.storage.storage_parts.desc_3: Storage parts can be removed from empty disks using &6[Shift-Right Click]&r with an empty disk in your hand.
quest.storage.storage_parts.desc_4: &7&oThis item is &cvery hard to craft&7 - consider &etrading&7, &elooting&7, or &escrapping&7 more advanced components instead.&r
quest.storage.storage_parts.title: Storage Parts
